calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Jun-09-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to Jun-09-2026 at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: D. Diaz; Not reported. (610)

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:49am and concluding at 9:55am.

Plaintiff Mark Ambort appearing represented by Michael Joseph Raifsnider, Esq. via zoom.
Defendant Mohamed Herbar not appearing.

The court reviews the Declaration in Support of Monetary Damages filed by the Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

Court examines Plaintiff MOHAMED HERBAR and he confirms statements made by counsel.

The matter is submitted.

Based on the documents filed, the statement made by Plaintiff and its Counsel, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Mark Ambort, and against Defendant Mohamed Herbar, as follows:

Damages: $75,000.00
Prejudgment Interest: $0
Attorney Fees: $0
Costs: $1064.95
Waiting Time Penalty: $0

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $76,064.95

Michael Reaifsnider is to prepare a judgment with these amounts and submit it to dept. 505 for review and signature.

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Continued To Apr-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505 - Matter Not Reported

Plaintiff Mark Ambort appeared via zoom with counsel Michael Joseph Raifsnider.
Defendant Mohamed Hebbar not appearing.

The court will continue this matter to allow Mr. Raifsnider an oppertunity to prepare and file a declaration from the Petitioner explaining in detail how he has been affected by the accident.

This matter is continued to April 22, 2026 at 9:00am in dept. 505.
